mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite falling unemployment, or monthly unemployment drops correlate with flat equity performance over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate capacity utilization and construction sector earnings, boosting forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over a 12-month rolling window, or INDPRO and HOUST diverge without corresponding SPX volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income combined with stable inflation expectations increases household consumption capacity, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain flat or negative when RSAFS rises and CPIAUCSL stabilizes, or the combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real disposable income signals sustained consumer purchasing power that directly supports retail and service sector earnings, generating positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly SPX returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth rates over a 3-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS months consistently yield negative equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production output reflects recovering manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and lifts corporate margins, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly higher following INDPRO acceleration episodes compared to baseline periods, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth occurring alongside a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that boosts aggregate demand and corporate hiring cycles, lifting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or employment growth decouples from equity performance during a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing construction activity serves as a leading indicator for broader economic momentum and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as construction and related sectors rally.
failure_condition: Weekly SPX returns remain flat or negative following HOUST acceleration spikes, or the housing indicator fails to lead equity price movements over a 2-year rolling evaluation window.
